note: Conservative rest-timer yoga suggestions. Use gentle options during strength rests; avoid intense end-range stretches under fatigue.

low-lunge.name: Low Lunge
low-lunge.cue: Keep the stretch light; breathe into the front of the hip.

half-forward-fold.name: Half Forward Fold
half-forward-fold.cue: Hands on thighs or bench; keep it easy, not maximal.

childs-pose-side-reach.name: Child’s Pose with Side Reach
childs-pose-side-reach.cue: Walk hands to one side and breathe into the side ribs.

supported-chest-opener.name: Supported Chest Opener
supported-chest-opener.cue: Use a doorway or bench; avoid cranking the shoulder.

thread-the-needle.name: Thread the Needle
thread-the-needle.cue: Rotate gently; do not force the shoulder.

figure-four.name: Figure Four Stretch
figure-four.cue: Keep the stretch mild and breathe.

downward-dog-calf-pedal.name: Downward Dog Calf Pedal
downward-dog-calf-pedal.cue: Pedal the heels one at a time rather than holding a deep stretch.

wrist-forearm-stretch.name: Wrist and Forearm Stretch
wrist-forearm-stretch.cue: Use light pressure only; stop if nerve symptoms appear.

overhead-triceps-stretch.name: Overhead Triceps Stretch
overhead-triceps-stretch.cue: Keep ribs down and shoulder relaxed.

constructive-rest.name: Constructive Rest Breathing
constructive-rest.cue: Lie or sit comfortably and breathe slowly through the nose.
